channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic Claude Fable 5 — Mythos but Safe, with Controversial Terms
item.description: The much anticipated launch of the Mythos-class model was marred by some controversial usage policies

item.title: Beyond the Solid-State Battery Myth: CAS Breaks Through on Multiple Fronts in 2026
item.description: Chinese Academy of Sciences delivers multiple battery breakthroughs in spring 2026 across black phosphorus fast-charging, safe sodium-ion, solid-state, and hydrogen storage technologies.

item.title: Huawei nova 16 Ultra Review: Kirin 9010S, 200MP Red Maple Camera, and 7000mAh Battery
item.description: Huawei's nova 16 Ultra brings a 200MP Red Maple camera, in-house Kirin 9010S chip, and 7000mAh battery in a 7.1mm body, marking the series' tenth anniversary with flagship-grade features.

item.title: Chinese Humanoid Robots Take the Internet by Storm as Overseas Influencers
item.description: Unitree Robotics' G1 humanoid robots go viral globally as a Buddhist monk in Korea, a counselor in Japan, a street celebrity in Poland, and a controversial influencer in the US.

item.title: CXMT and YMTC: Beyond the 'Hefei Myth' — How China's Memory Chip Giants Broke Through
item.description: CXMT's Q1 profit of RMB 24.76 billion and YMTC's STAR Market IPO guidance mark historic breakthroughs for China's memory chip industry, driven by AI demand and long-term industrial cultivation.

item.title: Generative AI Faces Its Fourth Bubble Debate: Three Cracks That Will Decide the Industry's Future
item.description: Generative AI faces its fourth major bubble debate as three structural cracks emerge in the market, while tech giants remain committed to massive infrastructure spending.

item.title: UPDATE: Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-06-09 11:22 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss. Description A fire at a third-party data center...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables
item.description: One step further into the power politics of frontier AI systems.

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and...

item.title: Fable not available in the model picker in Claude Code Desktop
item.description: Jun 9, 21:03 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. If you are experiencing issues with selecting Claude Fable within the Claude Code Desktop application, please ensure you have updated to the latest version. Jun 9, 20:16 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Jun 9, 20:32 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
Microsoft Patches 200 Vulnerabilities
item.description: Three of the vulnerabilities fixed with the latest Patch Tuesday updates were publicly disclosed before Microsoft addressed them. The post Microsoft Patches 200 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Adobe Patches 123 Vulnerabilities
item.description: Nearly half of the security holes, most allowing arbitrary code execution, have been fixed in Adobe’s Experience Manager product. The post Adobe Patches 123 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
item.description: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads. "Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to...

item.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Fable 5: Mythos-Class AI With Cybersecurity Guardrails
item.description: The AI giant also announced that Project Glasswing partners are being given access to the upgraded Mythos 5. The post Anthropic Launches Claude Fable 5: Mythos-Class AI With Cybersecurity Guardrails appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OpenSSL Patches High-Severity Vulnerability Found With AI
item.description: A total of 18 vulnerabilities have been patched in the latest OpenSSL releases, including many that were potentially discovered by AI. The post OpenSSL Patches High-Severity Vulnerability Found With AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
item.description: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0. "A vulnerability allowing remote code execution...

item.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
item.description: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code. "Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem,"...

item.title: Introducing North Mini Code: Cohere’s First Model For Developers
Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
item.description: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate brings near real-time, natural speech translation to Google AI Studio, Google Translate and Google Meet.

item.title: Claude Mythos Turns N-Days Into N-Hours With Rapid Exploit Creation
item.description: Public LLM models with safeguards turned off can also build working exploits, increasing patch gap risks. The post Claude Mythos Turns N-Days Into N-Hours With Rapid Exploit Creation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Hyperdrive and KV API errors
item.description: Jun 9, 14:37 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 9, 14:20 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue where some customers may experience latency or errors using Hyperdrive over Tunnels or the Workers KV API. We are working to analyze and...

item.title: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model
Powering the future of robotics in Europe
WinRAR Flaw Exploited by Russia-Aligned Groups to Deploy Stealers in Ukraine
item.description: Two Russia-aligned cyber attack campaigns have continued to exploit a security flaw in WinRAR to target Ukrainian organisations, almost a year after patches for the vulnerability were released. The activity has been attributed by Trend Micro to Earth Dahu (aka Gamaredon) and SHADOW-EARTH-066 (aka...

item.title: The Download: whole-body rejuvenation drugs and five things to know about AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in the XPrize competition The outspoken longevity scientist David Sinclair has predicted...

item.title: Researchers Build Self-Replicating AI Worm That Operates Entirely on Local, Open-Weight Models
item.description: University of Toronto researchers have built and tested a proof-of-concept AI-driven computer worm that uses a locally hosted open-weight large language model to reason its way through a network, generate tailored attack strategies for each target it encounters, and replicate itself, all without...

item.title: Multimedia Building Blocks
Cloudflare Workers and Pages build delays
item.description: Jun 9, 10:37 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with Cloudflare Workers and Pages build delays which potentially impacts multiple customers. Further detail will be provided as more information becomes available.

item.title: Learning to lead in a hybrid human-AI enterprise
item.description: As adoption of AI agents looks set to surge by as much as 300% in the next two years, leadership teams are carefully considering the implications of a hybrid human-AI workforce. Unlike existing enterprise-level automation that relies on manual input, AI agents are capable of autonomously...

item.title: David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in the XPrize competition
item.description: The outspoken longevity scientist David Sinclair has been predicting that one day, you’ll go to the doctor and get a prescription that will make you 10 years younger. Now MIT Technology Review has learned that he has plans to launch human tests of an oral “reprogramming” drug as part of...

item.title: Five things you need to know about AI
item.description: At SXSW London last week I gave a talk called “Five things you need to know about AI,” in which I shared what I think are the biggest themes in AI right now. I pulled a few things from our first AI10 list, an annual guide to the most important trends in this buzzy world,…

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Dubai
item.description: Jun 9, 07:30 UTC Resolved - Users in Dubai would have observed an increase in HTTP 5xx errors between 07:20 - 07:35 UTC 2026-06-09. This issue is now resolved.

item.title: GraphQL elevated latency
item.description: Jun 9, 07:15 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare identified an issue where some customers may have experienced latency querying the GraphQL API between 07:15 and 09:30 UTC.This has been resolved.

item.title: [AINews] FrontierCode: Benchmarking for Code Quality over Slop
item.description: We made a thing!

item.title: PathoSage: Towards Multi-Source Evidence Adjudication in Pathology via Experience-Aware Agentic Workflow
item.description: arXiv:2606.07549v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) and agent workflows have shown strong promise for computational pathology, yet reliable patch-level reasoning remains challenging. End-to-end pathology MLLMs often hallucinate morphological...

item.title: OmniMem: Perturbation-aware Memory Compression for Streaming Audio-Visual LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.07577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Audio-visual large language models (LLMs) hold strong promise for long-form video understanding, yet their long-video inference is fundamentally limited by the linear growth of video tokens and key-value (KV) caches. We present OmniMem, a...

item.title: Syll: Open-Source Personal Automation with Cross-Surface Execution
item.description: arXiv:2606.07594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personal AI agents must increasingly operate across APIs, shells, web surfaces, and desktop GUIs, yet many systems remain tuned to a single interface and offer limited support for user teaching and auditability. We present Syll, an open-source,...

item.title: A case study of evaluating AI agents on a neuroscience data-to-discovery pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2606.07718v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI tools offer a promising path to automating software development bottlenecks in scientific research pipelines, particularly for stages that take domain experts days to months to build, where scientists care about correctness and robustness,...

item.title: Why Limit the Residual Stream to Layers and Not Tokens? Persistent Memory for Continuous Latent Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2606.07720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable reasoning abilities on mathematical and multi-hop planning tasks. The CoCoNuT (Chain of Continuous Thought) paradigm~\cite{hao2024coconut} extends this by enabling models to reason in latent...

item.title: Offline Reinforcement Learning for Plasma Control in Nuclear Fusion: Codebase and Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2606.07550v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) offers a promising route for developing plasma controllers from historical tokamak data, since online trial-and-error on real devices is costly and risky. However, progress in this direction remains difficult to...

item.title: MedicalRec: Medical recommender system for image classification without retraining
item.description: arXiv:2606.07553v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The emergence of machine learning and deep learning has revolutionized the efficiency of diagnostic, therapeutic, and administrative systems in healthcare. However, this rapid adoption has come at the cost of requiring significant computing power and...

item.title: SPIN: Decentralized Swarm Control via Tensorized Policy Coordination
item.description: arXiv:2606.07557v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized multi-agent swarm coordination on resource-constrained edge platforms remains fundamentally bottlenecked by the exponential scaling of joint action spaces and high-latency communication overhead. This paper introduces the Swarm Policy...

item.title: Boundary Variance Inflation Causes Acquisition Bias in Gaussian Processes
item.description: arXiv:2606.07561v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gaussian processes with stationary kernels on bounded domains exhibit inflated posterior variance near the boundary. Despite being a long-recognized artifact in geostatistics and a source of over-exploration in Bayesian optimization, the causes and...

item.title: Emergence via Phase Transitions: Mechanism Landscapes and Universal Convergence Across Complex Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.07563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Across machine learning, biology, and physics, independently evolving systems often converge toward strikingly similar high-level structures despite radically different microscopic details. Grokking circuits converge across random seeds, evolutionary...

item.title: Bidirectional Small-Granularity Search between Code and Text
item.description: arXiv:2606.07519v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the novel task of bidirectional small-granularity search between code and text, where the queries are small snippets of text or code and the results are also small fragments of the opposite modality, i.e., code or text. This task...

item.title: TinyJudge: Unverifiable Constraint Alignment via Lightweight Specialist Ensembles
item.description: arXiv:2606.07520v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction Following (IF) is a core capability of LLMs, requiring strict adherence to diverse constraints, ranging from verifiable ones (e.g., output length) to unverifiable ones (e.g., tone). Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has...

item.title: Evaluating Hallucinations in Domain-Adapted Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.07521v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates the phenomenon of hallucinations in domain-adapted Large Language Models (LLMs), focusing on the fine-tuning of the Llama-2 model with the Lamini dataset. Hallucinations, or the generation of nonsensical or unfaithful content...

item.title: Community-Specific Slang and Entity Detection via Semantic Shift in Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.07522v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose an unsupervised method of resolving slang, unique entities, and folklore from online communities by isolating words in the lexicon that have the highest magnitude of semantic shift. Semantic shift is defined as the evolution of a word's...
